commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fb2d27556b12e7c8707921bcfcab9452ac6e4c8e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes model-call diagnostics to count streamed response bytes from chunks with accumulated `partial` snapshots removed, adds regression coverage, and updates logging/OTEL metric descriptions.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +104, Docs 0. Total +126 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main counts each streamed chunk with `JSON.stringify(chunk)`, while stream normalization emits chunks carrying accumulated `partial` snapshots, so repeated snapshots are included in `responseStreamBytes`. I did not rerun the Windows local-daemon starvation scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is needed to accept the compatibility-sensitive metric rebaseline; the remaining docs wording fix is mechanical but does not settle that approval.

target.title: fix(agents): count stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- fixes the diagnostics stream-byte counter introduced by `c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f` so plain stream deltas are counted from the new `delta` only
- avoids serializing full `partial` assistant snapshots on every streamed chunk, removing the repeated answer-so-far diagnostic work called out in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599#issuecomment-4555471713
- keeps diagnostic byte inspection best-effort so opaque or hostile chunk shapes cannot break the model stream

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: model-call diagnostics no longer repeatedly stringify full accumulated assistant partial snapshots for ordinary `text_delta`, `thinking_delta`, and `toolcall_delta` stream chunks.

Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a linked worktree, formatting/lint checks, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate. Blacksmith Testbox proof is still blocked locally by missing Blacksmith auth, so the broad remote gate used direct AWS Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --reporter=dot
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: refreshed head `1a1fbca30cd`; focused local wrapper passed `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` with 13/13 tests after rebasing onto current `origin/main`; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings with `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_8a1bea9b4348`, slug `jade-hermit`, run `run_b7ee3540ee57`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit `0`, `leaseStopped=true`. The changed gate selected lanes `core` and `coreTests`.

Observed result after fix: regression coverage proves diagnostic byte accounting does not call `toJSON` on full partial snapshots for text deltas, counts only incremental delta bytes, and keeps streaming alive when byte inspection cannot inspect a chunk.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This removes the diagnostics amplification from the starvation cluster but does not claim to close every related local-provider responsiveness path alone.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
